Q1.title: Multiplication Table
Q1.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print the multiplication table of N from 1 to 5, one result per line.

Example:
Input:  3
Output: 3
        6
        9
        12
        15

Q2.title: Even or Odd
Q2.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print Even if it is even, or Odd if it is odd.

Example:
Input:  4
Output: Even

Input:  7
Output: Odd

Q3.title: Sum of Numbers
Q3.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the sum of all numbers.

Example:
Input:  4
        1
        2
        3
        4
Output: 10

Q4.title: Count Even Numbers
Q4.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the count of even numbers.

Example:
Input:  6
        1
        2
        3
        4
        5
        6
Output: 3

Q5.title: Factorial
Q5.description:
Read a number N using 🎤, print its factorial.
Remember: 0! = 1

Example:
Input:  5
Output: 120

Q6.title: Reverse String
Q6.description:
Read a string using 🎤, print it reversed.

Tip: Convert string to list, reverse using ↩️, then join with 🧩

Example:
Input:  HELLO
Output: OLLEH

Q7.title: Find Maximum
Q7.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the maximum value.

Tip: Use 🔺 to find max of a list

Example:
Input:  5
        3
        8
        2
        7
        5
Output: 8

Q8.title: Two Sum Exists
Q8.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Then read a target number T.
Print Yes if any two distinct numbers in the list add up to T, otherwise print No.

Tip: Use a dictionary 🗂️ to store numbers you have seen. For each number x, check if (T - x) is already in the dict.

Example:
Input:  5
        2
        7
        11
        15
        3
        9
Output: Yes
(2 + 7 = 9)

Q9.title: Squares of Sorted Array
Q9.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers (may have negatives). Print the square of each number in sorted order, one per line.

Tip: Square each number using 🌟 2, sort using 🗃️

Example:
Input:  4
        -2
        0
        1
        3
Output: 0
        1
        4
        9

Q10.title: Binary Search
Q10.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers in sorted ascending order. Then read a target T.
Print the index (0-based) where T is found, or -1 if not found.
You must implement binary search — do NOT use list find or loop through all elements.

Tip: Use low 📦 0, high 📦 N-1, mid 📦 (low ➕ high) 🔢 2. Loop while low 🔛 high.

Example:
Input:  6
        1
        3
        5
        7
        9
        11
        7
Output: 3

Q11.title: Count Digits
Q11.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print the number of digits in it.

Example:
Input:  12345
Output: 5

Q12.title: Count Vowels
Q12.description:
Read a string using 🎤. Print the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u — both upper and lower case).

Example:
Input:  Hello World
Output: 3

Q13.title: Sum of Digits
Q13.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print the sum of its digits.

Example:
Input:  1234
Output: 10

Q14.title: Palindrome Check
Q14.description:
Read a string using 🎤. Print Yes if it is a palindrome, otherwise print No. (Case sensitive)

Example:
Input:  racecar
Output: Yes

Input:  hello
Output: No

Q15.title: Fibonacci Series
Q15.description:
Read N using 🎤. Print the first N Fibonacci numbers, one per line, starting from 0.

Example:
Input:  6
Output: 0
        1
        1
        2
        3
        5

Q16.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
Q16.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Find the length of the longest sequence of consecutive integers in the list. Order does not matter.

Tip: Store all numbers in a dict 🗂️. For each number, check if (number - 1) is NOT in the dict — that means it is the start of a sequence. Then count how far the sequence goes.

Example:
Input:  8
        100
        4
        200
        1
        3
        2
        5
        101
Output: 5
(1,2,3,4,5 is the longest consecutive sequence)

Q17.title: Prime Check
Q17.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print Yes if it is prime, otherwise print No.

Example:
Input:  7
Output: Yes

Input:  9
Output: No

Q18.title: majority Element
Q18.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Find the element that appears more than N/2 times and print it. It is guaranteed that such an element always exists.

Tip: Use a dictionary 🗂️ to count frequency of each number. Then find which key has count greater than N // 2.

Example:
Input:  7
        3
        2
        3
        1
        3
        4
        3
Output: 3

Q19.title: Run Length Encoding
Q19.description:
Read a string using 🎤. Compress it using run-length encoding — for each group of consecutive identical characters, print the count followed by the character, all on one line.

Example:
Input:  AAABBC
Output: 3A2B1C

Input:  AABAA
Output: 2A1B2A

Q20.title: Valid Parentheses
Q20.description:
Read a string using 🎤 containing only ( and ) characters. Print Yes if the parentheses are valid (every opening bracket has a matching closing bracket in the right order), otherwise print No.

Tip: Use a counter starting at 0. Add 1 for ( and subtract 1 for ). If counter ever goes below 0, it is invalid. At the end it must be 0.

Example:
Input:  (())
Output: Yes

Input:  )(
Output: No

Input:  (()
Output: No
